$schema_note: SAMPLE / PARTIAL / DUAL-DIALECT. Hand-authored from the AnimBP2FP exporter's OWN evidence. Format identical to matlang.sample.json: lang -> nodeType -> {classPath?, properties:{name:{type,default?}}, pins:{name:{required:bool}}}. KEY-RESOLUTION DIFFERS from matlang: animlang node heads ARE their own name (head==name), so enrich never promotes attrs.node_type; the animlang semantic pass keys the schema on r['name'] for kind=='node', NOT attrs.node_type.
$dialect_caveat: TWO DISTINCT DIALECTS share the .animlang extension. (A) DESIGN-SKETCH form = the in-repo DSL/Examples/*.animlang + Extras/Tools/animlang-lint.rkt + Docs/README.md (node names blendspace-1d/-2d, aimoffset-2d, two-bone-ik, layered-blend-per-bone, reference-pose; positional pose operands for blend; (state ..)/(transition ..) list forms). (B) REAL EXPORTER form = AnimBPExporter.cpp + Extras/Tools/animlang-nodes.rkt (blendspace-player, two-bone-i-k via CamelToKebab, aim-offset, layered-bone-blend; transitions serialized as a STRING property). The enrich .scm + the existing example-zero-error tests are calibrated to dialect (A), so this dict is GROUNDED IN DIALECT (A) (its node names carry no 'external' flag) and ADDITIVELY includes dialect-(B) node names + the few divergent ones marked 'external': true. The two dialects disagree on transition/rule-graph representation, so transition-as-string and rule-graph handling are NOT modeled — flagged external.
$pin_model_note: animlang pose inputs are NOT (connect ...) (that is matlang-only). In dialect (A) pose operands are sometimes POSITIONAL (blend's two children) and sometimes :keyword-named (layered-blend-per-bone :base, slot :default, blend-layer :source). Because the form is inconsistent and the matlang precedent reserves required:true only for no-fallback pins, ALL pose-input pins here are required:false (the animlang semantic pass treats a :keyword whose value is a child node-list as a connected pin, but never errors on a missing one). Required-ness is hand-curated and intentionally absent. Prefer UNKNOWN_NODE_TYPE warnings over false MISSING_REQUIRED_PIN errors.
$strict_props_note: UNKNOWN_PROP is OFF by default and fires ONLY for forms that declare "strict-props": true. The design-sketch dialect uses LEADING POSITIONAL KEYWORD IDENTIFIERS — a state-machine's name (state-machine :locomotion ..), a state's name (state :idle ..), a transition's from/to (transition :idle :walk ..) — which are structurally indistinguishable from :key value props, so prop-checking those forms would false-warn on every user-chosen state name. strict-props is therefore set ONLY on forms whose entire keyword surface is a CLOSED, fully-enumerated set with NO positional keyword args (sequence-player, blend, blendspace-1d, blendspace-2d, aimoffset-2d, blend-layer, notify). state-machine / state / transition deliberately omit it. This keeps UNKNOWN_PROP a true signal (typo'd prop on a closed-vocab leaf) instead of noise.

anim-blueprint.$node_note: Root form (anim-blueprint "Name" :variables [..] :anim-graph <node> :layers [..] :anim-notifies [..]). lint.rkt check-blueprint-body. :variables/:layers/:anim-notifies values are vectors handled by the .scm head-filter, not nodes; :anim-graph holds the pose-tree root.

sequence-player.$node_note: (sequence-player "Anim" :loop true :play-rate ..). Leaf node, closed keyword surface -> strict-props. lint.rkt + nodes.rkt UAnimGraphNode_SequencePlayer. :name is the exporter-form key; the design-sketch positions the anim name positionally.

blend.$node_note: (blend <alpha> <pose1> <pose2>) design-sketch positional OR (blend :alpha .. :a <pose> :b <pose>) exporter. UAnimGraphNode_TwoWayBlend. Pose operands required:false (positional in dialect A). strict-props: keyword surface is closed (:alpha/:alpha-curve-name + :a/:b pins); positional poses carry no keyword so they never trip prop-check.

blendspace-1d.$node_note: (blendspace-1d "Name" :axis :param :loop true). Dialect-(A) lint.rkt. Exporter emits blendspace-player instead. strict-props: :axis/:loop/:play-rate closed surface (the :axis value is a keyword param-ref, consumed as the value of :axis).

blendspace-2d.$node_note: (blendspace-2d "Name" :x :param :y :param :loop true :play-rate ..). Dialect-(A) lint.rkt + third_person_char.animlang. strict-props: closed :x/:y/:loop/:play-rate surface.

state-machine.$node_note: Dialect (A): (state-machine :name-kw :initial :state :states [(state ..)] :transitions [(transition ..)]). Exporter (B) emits :name/:initial + :transitions as a serialized STRING — that representation is NOT modeled (external split). Dialect-(A) keys listed.

state.$node_note: (state :name <anim> [:transition-to :other :on-finish true]). lint.rkt check-state + third_person_char.animlang. The state animation is a positional child pose.

transition.$node_note: (transition :from :to :condition <expr> :duration .. :priority .. :from-states [..]). Dialect-(A) lint.rkt check-transition. :from/:to are leading state-ref keywords; :condition is an expression (handled by the .scm, not a node). external: exporter (B) serializes transitions as a string, so this list-form is dialect-(A)-only.

layered-blend-per-bone.$node_note: (layered-blend-per-bone :base <pose> :layers [(blend-layer ..)]). Dialect-(A) lint.rkt + third_person_char.animlang. :base is a named pose input.

layered-blend.$node_note: (layered-blend :base <pose> :additive <pose>). Dialect-(A) third_person_char.animlang (nested under a blend-layer :source). Both poses named, optional.

blend-layer.$node_note: (blend-layer :name .. :weight <expr> :blend-mode :blend|:replace :bone-filter "bone" :blend-depth N :source <pose>). Dialect-(A) third_person_char.animlang + types.rkt BlendLayer. strict-props: closed surface; :blend-mode's keyword value (:blend/:replace) is consumed as the value of :blend-mode.

aimoffset-2d.$node_note: (aimoffset-2d "Name" :x :param :y :param). Dialect-(A) third_person_char.animlang. Exporter (B) emits aim-offset. strict-props: closed :x/:y surface.

slot.$node_note: (slot "SlotName" :default <pose>) design-sketch OR (slot :slot-name .. :source <pose>) exporter. UAnimGraphNode_Slot. third_person_char.animlang uses :default.

reference-pose.$node_note: (reference-pose) — T-pose leaf. Dialect-(A) third_person_char.animlang. Exporter (B) emits identity-pose.

two-bone-ik.$node_note: (two-bone-ik :effector-location <expr> :joint-target <expr>). Dialect-(A) third_person_char.animlang. Exporter (B) emits two-bone-i-k (CamelToKebab splits I/K).

get-socket-location.$node_note: (get-socket-location "Target" "Socket") — value helper used inside two-bone-ik args. Dialect-(A) third_person_char.animlang.

notify.$node_note: (notify "Name" :callback handler). Dialect-(A) lint.rkt check-anim-notify + third_person_char.animlang :anim-notifies. strict-props: only :callback.

blend-list.$node_note: Exporter-form (B). (blend-list :class "AnimGraphNode_BlendListBy.." :active-* <param> :blend-time_N .. :blend-pose_N <pose>). Dynamic pose pins (blend-pose_N) not enumerable -> none declared. nodes.rkt.

apply-mesh-space-additive.$node_note: Exporter-form (B). UAnimGraphNode_ApplyMeshSpaceAdditive. Same shape as apply-additive. nodes.rkt.

layered-bone-blend.$node_note: Exporter-form (B). (layered-bone-blend :blend-weights_N .. :base-pose <pose> :blend-poses_N <pose>). Dynamic blend-poses_N not enumerable. nodes.rkt.

two-bone-i-k.$node_note: Exporter-form (B), CamelToKebab of UAnimGraphNode_TwoBoneIK splits I/K. (two-bone-i-k :alpha <param> :component-pose <pose>). nodes.rkt.

aim-offset.$node_note: Exporter-form (B), CamelToKebab. (aim-offset :base-pose <pose>). Dialect (A) calls this aimoffset-2d. nodes.rkt.

identity-pose.$node_note: Exporter-form (B). (identity-pose) — T-pose/identity leaf. Dialect (A) calls this reference-pose. nodes.rkt.

root.$node_note: Exporter-form (B). AnimGraphNode result root. AnimBPExporter.cpp hardcoded type.
